entries.title: Python Tutorial - W3Schools
entries.summary:
* Python syntax is used to define the structure of Python code.
* Variables in Python are defined using the `=` operator, e.g., `x = 5`.
* Python data types include Numbers, Strings, Booleans, Lists, Tuples, Sets, and Dictionaries.
* Python operators include Arithmetic, Assignment, Comparison, Logical, Identity, and Membership operators.
* Control structures in Python include If-Else statements, For loops, and While loops.
* Functions in Python are defined using the `def` keyword, e.g., `def greet():`.
* Modules in Python are used to organize and reuse code, imported using the `import` statement.
* Python supports object-oriented programming (OOP) concepts, including Classes and Objects.
* Error handling in Python is done using Try-Except blocks, e.g., `try: ... except: ...`.
* Python has built-in support for regular expressions (RegEx) and JSON data format.
* Python supports object-oriented programming (OOP) concepts like classes, objects, inheritance, polymorphism, and encapsulation.
* Python has built-in data structures like lists, stacks, queues, linked lists, hash tables, trees, and graphs.
* Python supports file handling operations like reading, writing, and deleting files.
* Python has libraries like NumPy, Pandas, and SciPy for scientific computing and data analysis.
* Python has frameworks like Django for web development and Matplotlib for data visualization.
* Python supports machine learning concepts like linear regression, polynomial regression, and decision trees

* OpenCV is released under the Apache 2 License and is free for commercial use.
* The library is highly optimized for real-time applications.
* OpenCV supports C++, Python, and Java interfaces.
* Cross-platform support includes Linux, MacOS, Windows, iOS, and Android.
* OpenCV has a deep learning module for AI and computer vision tasks.
* The library includes tools for image and video manipulation, object and face detection.
* OpenCV provides a range of resources, including tutorials, documentation, and a Q&A forum.
* OpenCV is widely used in various applications, including self-driving cars, robotics, and human behavior analysis.
* OpenCV supports C++, Python, and Java interfaces
* Cross-platform compatibility: Linux, MacOS, Windows, iOS, and Android
* Integrated with CUDA for GPU acceleration
* Supported by ARM for embedded systems
* Interfaces with deep learning frameworks: PyTorch, TensorFlow, and Keras

* Official resources include books, podcasts, and web stories
* OpenCV has a GitHub repository for community development
* Partners with Intel as a Platinum Member
* Released under an open-source license

entries.title: FFmpeg
entries.summary:
* FFmpeg is a cross-platform solution for recording, converting, and streaming audio and video.
* FFmpeg 8.1 "Hoare" is the latest minor release, featuring new decoders, formats, and filters.
* New decoders include xHE-AAC, MPEG-H, and LCEVC, with support for parsing and forwarding metadata.
* Vulkan compute-based codecs support ProRes encoding and decoding, DPX decoding, and FFv1 encoding and decoding.
* D3D12 support includes H.264/AV1 encoding, scale_d3d12, mestimate_d3d12, and deinterlace_d3d12 filters.
* Rockchip H.264/HEVC hardware encoding is supported.
* New formats include hxvs demuxer, and new filters include drawvg and vpp_amf.
* FFmpeg 8.0 "Huffman" introduced native decoders for APV, ProRes RAW, and RealVideo 6.0.
* Hardware accelerated decoding and encoding are supported for various codecs, including Vulkan VP9 and VAAPI VVC.
* FFmpeg supports FFv1, ProRes RAW, ProRes, and VC-2 codecs.
* New codecs support parallelized decoding for significant speedups.
* VVC decoder is now stable after maturing in version 7.0.
* Native AAC USAC decoder is added for extensive volume normalization metadata.
* MV-HEVC decoding is supported for stereoscopic coding.
* LC-EVC decoding is supported via an external library.
* Vulkan encoding is supported for H264 and HEVC with feature-parity with VAAPI.
* AV1 encoding support is coming soon to Vulkan with planned day-one support in FFmpeg.
* Full-range image support is improved with correct and consistent color range forwarding.
* FFmp

* The project uses multiple licenses, including GPL-2.0, GPL-3.0, LGPL-2.1, and LGPL-3.0

* The project has 60.4k stars, 1.5k watchers, and 13.8k forks on GitHub

* The project's primary topics include audio, video, streaming, and multimedia
* FFmpeg uses FFT and HEVC technologies
* The project has a large community of contributors, with an error occurring while loading the contri

entries.title: FFmpeg - Wikipedia
entries.summary:
* FFmpeg is a free and open-source software project for handling video, audio, and multimedia files and streams.
* Written in C and Assembly, with support for various operating systems, including Windows, macOS, and Linux.
* Supports multiple platforms, including x86, ARM, PowerPC, MIPS, RISC-V, and others.
* Includes libraries such as libavcodec, libavformat, and libavfilter for audio/video processing and editing.
* Command-line tools include ffmpeg, ffplay, and ffprobe for processing, playing, and analyzing media files.
* Supports transcoding, editing, video scaling, and post-production effects.
* Published under LGPL-2.1-or-later or GPL-2.0-or-later licenses.
* Includes encoders and decoders for many audio and video file formats.
* Part of the workflow of many software projects, including VLC, YouTube, and Bilibili.
* FFmpeg was started by Fabrice Bellard in 2000 and has undergone significant development and forks, including the Libav project.
* FFmpeg publishes a new release every three months on average.
* FFmpeg developers recommend compiling the software from source using the latest build from their source code with Git.
* FFmpeg has created video codecs FFV1 and Snow, and container format NUT.
* FFmpeg supports various formats, including WebM, VP8, VP9, HEVC, AAC, and AV1.
* FFmpeg has command-line tools: ffmpeg, ffplay, and ffprobe.

* FFmpeg supports image formats,

entries.title: How to Install FFmpeg on Windows: A Step-by-Step Guide - wikiHow
entries.summary:
* FFmpeg is an open-source media tool for converting video formats.
* It is a command-line only tool, with no graphical interface.
* FFmpeg can convert any video format into the desired one.
* Installation on Windows requires downloading and configuring the tool.
* FFmpeg has no graphical, clickable interface, unlike regular Windows programs.
* The tool is often used for audio and video conversion.
* Command-line expertise is required to use FFmpeg effectively.
* FFmpeg is compatible with Windows operating systems.
* The tool can be used for various media processing tasks, including video and audio conversion.
* FFmpeg is a free and open-source software.
* Install 7-Zip to extract FFmpeg files.
* Download FFmpeg from www.gyan.dev/ffmpeg/builds.
* Extract FFmpeg folder and move it to the root of the main drive.
* Add the FFmpeg folder to the system path.
* Use `winget install -e --id Gyan.FFmpeg --exact` to install FFmpeg via command prompt.
* Download `ffmpeg-release-full.7z` for Windows 10 and later.
* Download `ffmpeg-release-essentials.7z` for Windows 7 and 8.
* Use 7-Zip to extract the downloaded FFmpeg file.
* Rename the extracted folder to "FFmpeg".
* Use Command Prompt as an administrator to verify FFmpeg installation.
* To install FFmpeg, move the folder to the root of the hard drive (e.g., `C:\ffmpeg\bin`).
* Open Command Prompt as an administrator to add FFmpeg to the system path.
* Use the command `setx /m PATH "C:\ffmpeg\bin;%PATH%"` to add FFmpeg to the system pa

entries.title: Automate Video Processing with FFmpeg and Python | 32blog
entries.summary:
* FFmpeg is called from Python using `subprocess.run()` with the command as a list of strings.
* `ffmpeg-python` library is a convenient wrapper but adds a dependency and hasn't been updated since 2019.
* `MAX_WORKERS` should match available CPU cores to avoid contention.
* 2-4 workers is a reasonable starting point for parallel encoding on an 8-core machine.
* Memory usage scales linearly with the number of parallel encodes.
* File I/O can become a bottleneck, especially on spinning disks.
* Using 4 workers can deliver a 3-4x speedup over single-threaded processing for large batches.
* Offloading work to a VPS encoding server can be beneficial for very large batches.
* `python-ffmpeg` and `typed-ffmpeg` are alternative libraries to `ffmpeg-python`.

* The toolkit provides video information probing for encoding details and duration.

entries.title: Automated Video Processing with FFmpeg and Docker - img.ly
entries.summary:
* Job queue system manages video processing tasks asynchronously with state persistence.
* Job Class represents a video processing task with attributes like id, input_file, output_file, operation, and status.
* JobQueue Class manages a queue of video processing jobs with asynchronous workers and tracks key statistics.
* VideoProcessor class provides methods for video processing using FFmpeg, including transcoding, compression, and metadata extraction.
* Video operations supported: transcode, compress, add_watermark, generate_thumbnail, extract_audio, create_gif, concatenate_videos, and trim_video.
* FFmpeg execution is handled by the _execute_ffmpeg method with progress tracking and error handling.
* ConfigManager class manages video processing profiles and workflows stored in a YAML configuration file.
* Configuration Management loads profiles and workflows from a YAML file and saves updates or new data.
* Asynchronous workers process jobs using a processor object, updating progress and handling errors.
* Subprocesses are used to interact with FFmpeg, supporting progress callbacks for real-time updates.
* FFmpeg is used for automated video processing
* Docker is utilized for building a production-ready batch video processing server
* AWS Spot Instances can be used for scalable and low-cost video processing with FFmpeg
* FFmpeg can be used for cropping and trimming videos
* FFmpeg can be integrated with Flutter for video processing
* Docker provides a containerized environmen

entries.title: OpenCV Video Processing: A Comprehensive Guide - CodeRivers
entries.summary:
* OpenCV treats videos as a sequence of frames, where each frame is an image.
* `VideoCapture` object is used to read frames from a video file or live video source.
* `VideoWriter` object is used to write processed frames to a new video file.
* `cv2.VideoCapture` is used to create a `VideoCapture` object to read the input video.
* `cap.read()` function returns a boolean `ret` and the frame itself, indicating whether the frame was read successfully.
* `cv2.VideoWriter` is used to create a `VideoWriter` object, specifying output file name, codec, frame rate, and frame size.
* `out.write(frame)` is used to write each frame to the output video.
* Frames can be manipulated using various OpenCV functions, such as converting to grayscale using `cv2.cvtColor(frame, cv2.COLOR_BGR2GRAY)`.
* `cv2.imshow` is used to display the video frames, and `cv2.waitKey` is used to handle keyboard events.
* `cap.release()` and `out.release()` are used to release the `VideoCapture` and `VideoWriter` objects, respectively.

entries.title: OpenCV Tutorial in Python - GeeksforGeeks
entries.summary:
* OpenCV is an open-source computer vision and machine learning library.
* OpenCV supports image and video processing, object detection, and handwriting recognition.
* Key features include comprehensive image processing, real-time video processing, and cross-platform compatibility.
* OpenCV integrates with deep learning libraries like TensorFlow and PyTorch.
* Essential OpenCV functions include loading, saving, and displaying images.
* Image processing techniques include blurring, grayscaling, scaling, rotating, and edge detection.
* Image segmentation techniques include simple thresholding, adaptive thresholding, and Otsu thresholding.
* Morphological operations include erosion, dilation, bilateral filtering, and denoising.
* OpenCV supports color space conversion and histogram equalization.
* OpenCV can be installed on Windows, Linux, macOS, and Android platforms.

entries.title: Public Image Ltd - Wikipedia
entries.summary:
* Public Image Ltd's album "The Flowers of Romance" features minimal bass parts and emphasizes synthesizers, drums, and tape loops.
* Martin Atkins' drumming style on the album is described as "marching band"-style.
* The album incorporates musique concrète and tape loops, with Levene playing some bass and keyboards.
* The track "Francis Massacre" was inspired by Lydon's incarceration in Mountjoy Prison.
* The album "Commercial Zone" was recorded in 1982 but released later, with contributions from bassist Pete Jones.
* PiL's live album "Live in Tokyo" (1983) was one of the first digital live albums ever recorded.
* The album "This Is What You Want... This Is What You Get" (1984) features re-recorded versions of songs from "Commercial Zone" and new tracks with a horn section.
* PiL's single "This Is Not a Love Song" (1983) reached No. 5 in the UK singles charts and No. 12 in the Netherlands.
* The band's sound shifted towards commercial pop and dance music during this period, alienating some of their original audience.
* The song "This Is Not a Love Song" was inspired by a line from the Flying Lizards' song "Her Story", which critiques bands for "selling out" their artistic principles.

entries.title: Python Pillow - Batch Processing of Images - Online Tutorials Library
entries.summary:
* Python Pillow supports batch processing of images for efficient editing.
* Batch processing can be used for resizing, cropping, renaming, watermarking, and formatting images.
* It enhances workflow efficiency and optimizes output, especially with large numbers of images.
* Pillow's batch processing can significantly impact speed and efficiency.
* Batch processing can be applied to various image operations like flipping, rotating, and adding borders.
* ImageDraw module can be used for writing text on images during batch processing.
* Batch processing can also be used for image filtering, such as blurring, edge detection, and embossing.
* Pillow's batch processing can handle image sequences and color conversions.
* Batch processing can be used for image enhancement and correction, such as enhancing contrast and sharpness.
* Pillow's batch processing can be automated using Python scripts for efficient image editing.

entries.title: Image Processing With the Python Pillow Library
entries.summary:
* The `rotate()` method in Pillow rotates an image by a specified angle counterclockwise.
* The `expand` parameter in `rotate()` can be set to `True` to return a larger image that fully contains the rotated image.
* An image in Pillow is a 2D array of pixels, where each pixel corresponds to a color.
* Each pixel can be represented by one or more values (e.g., RGB, RGBA).
* The `getbands()` method returns the number of bands in an image (e.g., 'R', 'G', 'B' for RGB).
* The `convert()` method converts an image from one mode to another (e.g., RGB to CMYK or grayscale).
* Supported modes in Pillow include binary, grayscale, RGB, RGBA, and CMYK.
* The mode of an image describes the type of image (e.g., RGB, CMYK).
* RGBA images have four bands: red, green, blue, and alpha (transparency).
* Image modes can be converted using the `convert()` method (e.g., `img.convert('CMYK')`).

entries.title: What is OpenCV Library? - GeeksforGeeks
entries.summary:
* OpenCV is used for object detection and recognition.
* Haar Cascades and YOLO v2 are algorithms used for object detection in OpenCV.
* R-CNN and Mask R-CNN are Region-Based Convolutional Neural Networks for object detection.
* Image segmentation techniques include Thresholding, Region and Edge Based Segmentation, and U-Net.
* OpenCV supports face recognition using Eigenfaces (PCA Algorithm) and Deep Face Recognition.
* Detection Transformer (DETR) is used for object detection.
* OpenCV provides tools for camera calibration and pose estimation.
* Stereo images can be used to create depth maps with OpenCV.
* Watershed Algorithm is used for image segmentation in OpenCV.

entries.title: FFmpeg filter_complex patterns — branching, merging, and multi …
entries.summary:
* FFmpeg `filter_complex` enables non-linear filter graphs with multiple inputs, outputs, branching, and merging.
* The labeling system uses square brackets to identify streams: `[INDEX:STREAM_TYPE]` for input streams and `[NAME]` for intermediate streams.
* Filters take labeled inputs and produce labeled outputs, with the graph described as a series of filter operations separated by semicolons.
* The `split` filter creates multiple copies of a stream, allowing for branching and multiple outputs.
* The `concat`, `hstack`, and `overlay` filters can be used for merging multiple streams into one output.
* Audio and video can be processed jointly using `filter_complex`.
* Hardware acceleration can be used with `filter_complex` for improved performance.
* Debugging `filter_complex` graphs can be challenging, but understanding the labeling system and filter syntax is key.
* Common use cases for `filter_complex` include multi-output pipelines, audio/video joint processing, and cross-stream processing.
* FFmpeg's `split` filter creates multiple copies of a stream for parallel processing.
* `overlay` filter overlays one video on top of another with customizable positioning.
* `hstack` filter stacks multiple videos horizontally for side-by-side comparison.
* `amix` filter mixes multiple audio streams with adjustable volume and duration.
* `enable` expression in `overlay` filter controls the timing of overlay appearance.
* `split` and `map` filters enable multiple output files with diff

entries.title: ffmpeg-python: Python bindings for FFmpeg - GitHub Pages
entries.summary:
* `me_num` variable default value is 0
* `tabsize` default value is 4
* `timecode` format is "hh:mm:ss[:;.]ff"
* `timecode_rate` is an alias for `rate` and `r`
* `tc24hmax` default value is 0 (disabled)
* `text` and `textfile` parameters are mutually exclusive
* `x` and `y` expressions can use constants like `dar`, `hsub`, `vsub`, `line_h`, `main_h`, `main_w`, `ascent`, `descent`
* `rand(min, max)` function returns a random number between `min` and `max`
* `sar` is the sample aspect ratio
* `hsub` and `vsub` are horizontal and vertical chroma subsample values
* `th` and `text_h` represent the height of rendered text.
* `tw` and `text_w` represent the width of rendered text.
* `x` and `y` represent the offset coordinates where the text is drawn.
* `sar` represents the input sample aspect ratio.
* `t` represents the timestamp in seconds.
* `filter_` function applies a custom filter to a stream, taking `stream_spec`, `filter_name`, `*args`, and `**kwargs` as parameters.
* `filter_multi_output` function applies a custom filter with one or more outputs.
* `hflip` function flips the input video horizontally.
* `hue` function modifies the hue and/or saturation of the input, taking `h`, `s`, `H`, and `b` as parameters.
* `overlay` function overlays one video on top of another, taking `x` and `eof_action` as parameters.

entries.title: FFmpeg Complete Guide: Installation, Commands & Use Cases - FFmpeg A…
entries.summary:
* FFmpeg is a powerful tool for handling multimedia data.
* Supported input file: `input.mp4`.
* Supported output file: `output.mp4`.
* Command for adding subtitles: `ffmpeg -i input.mp4 -vf "subtitles=subtitles.srt" output.mp4`.
* FFmpeg can stream live video over the internet.
* Supported subtitle file: `subtitles.srt`.
* FFmpeg supports a wide range of codecs and formats.
* Basic syntax: `ffmpeg [options] [[input_file] output_file]`.
* Option `-i` specifies the input file.
* Option `-vf` specifies a video filter, such as adding subtitles.

entries.title: Top Python Packages for Realistic Text-to-Speech Solutions
entries.summary:
* Python is a popular choice for text-to-speech (TTS) applications due to its simplicity, flexibility, and AI capabilities.
* Key Python libraries for TTS include TensorFlow, PyTorch, and WaveGlow.
* Popular Python packages for realistic TTS are pyttsx3, Coqui TTS, Mimic3, and Orca TTS.
* Python offers easy integration with deep learning frameworks for advanced voice synthesis.
* Python-based TTS applications support multiple languages and accents.
* Real-time processing capabilities are available with some Python-based TTS solutions.
* Cloud providers like Google Cloud, Amazon Polly, and Smallest.ai offer Python SDKs for TTS integration.
* Python's cross-platform compatibility allows TTS applications to run on Windows, macOS, Linux, and embedded systems.
* Python's simple syntax makes it easy for beginners and experienced developers to implement TTS solutions quickly.
* Smallest.ai Waves is a Python-based TTS solution that generates speech in under 100 milliseconds.

entries.title: A guide on converting Text to Speech with Python - Murf
entries.summary:
* Python libraries for text-to-speech (TTS) include pyttsx3, gTTS, and Amazon Polly.
* pyttsx3 is a cross-platform TTS library that supports multiple TTS engines.
* gTTS (Google Text-to-Speech) is a library for converting text to speech using Google's TTS API.
* Amazon Polly is a cloud-based TTS service that provides high-quality, lifelike voices.
* Offline TTS engines are reliable but robotic, while cloud-based TTS provides realistic voices but requires an internet connection.
* Python's simple syntax and readable code make it easy to implement speech synthesis.
* TTS works best when combined with other Python tools like NLP, web apps, or machine learning.
* Cross-platform support means Python-based TTS works consistently on Windows, macOS, and Linux.
* Choosing the right TTS library is crucial for ensuring desired functionality and performance.
* Murf is a service that provides natural, human-like speech for Python projects.
* Murf offers a Text-to-Speech API for converting text to speech
* Supported use cases include e-learning, advertisements, explainer videos, and audiobooks
* Murf provides voice styles, accent generation, and voice cloning capabilities
* The platform supports multilingual dubbing and transcription
* Integrations are available for Canva, Google Slides, and PowerPoint
* Murf offers a studio for creating and editing audio content
* The platform provides a savings calculator and TTS benchmarks
* Murf supports conversational AI voice and speech-to-text conve
